title: [Seq] invalid register opt leads to comb loop
body:
h/t @dtzSiFive 
The first example from [3003](https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/3003) results in a cycle that we spin infinitely on in HW,
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Test:
  public module Test:
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : UInt<1>
    output o : UInt<8>
    wire w : UInt<8>
    reg r : UInt<8>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, mux(reset, UInt<8>(3), w))
    w <= r
    o <= r
```
Before canonicalize in HW this is now:

```mlir
hw.module @Test(in %clock : !seq.clock, in %reset : i1, out o : i8) {
  %c3_i8 = hw.constant 3 : i8
  %w = hw.wire %r  : i8
  %0 = comb.mux bin %reset, %c3_i8, %w : i8
  %r = seq.firreg %r clock %clock reset sync %reset, %0 {firrtl.random_init_start = 0 : ui64} : i8
  hw.output %r : i8
}
```
Which canonicalize quickly reduces the register to a combinational loop
```mlir
hw.module @Test(in %clock : !seq.clock, in %reset : i1, out o : i8) {
  %c3_i8 = hw.constant 3 : i8
  %0 = comb.mux bin %reset, %c3_i8, %0 : i8
  hw.output %0 : i8
}
```

The register should not be optimized in this way.  We had a similar issue in the folder which was fixed (h/t @uenoku) https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/7085.

The issue appears to be in this code:
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/cc51aa5dd03ca451e0329c4a5552565b106c0c0f/lib/Dialect/Seq/SeqOps.cpp#L571-L582
